summary: DHH enthusiastically reviews his Nokia 3650, praising its versatile feature set including camera, email, retro gaming emulators, and mobile browsing. He highlights Apple's new iSync 1.1 compatibility for address book synchronization and QuickTime 6.3's ability to play back the phone's video recordings on Mac. The post captures the early excitement of smartphone convergence, when a single device combining camera, email, and web browsing felt revolutionary. DHH predicts mobile movie-blogging will take off thanks to the new QuickTime 3GPP plugin.
key_points: The Nokia 3650 represented early smartphone convergence: camera, email, gaming, calendar, and web browsing in one device for $300
Apple's iSync 1.1 added address book synchronization with the Nokia 3650, with calendar sync promised soon
QuickTime 6.3's 3GPP plugin freed phone-recorded video from Nokia's Windows-only player, enabling playback on Mac
DHH saw mobile video recording and playback as a catalyst for 'mobile movie-blogging'
money_quotes: There's just so much to love.
All in a single, slick package for around $300.
And now Apple just blessed it with iSync-compatibility!
The movies recorded from the camera phone can be played-back outside of Nokia's closed, Windows-based player. That is freaking awesome.
Prepare to see mobile movie-blogging rocket.
The Nokia 3650 is the phone to own.
key_insight: Even in 2003, the convergence of camera, email, gaming, and web browsing into a single mobile device was generating excitement about the future of mobile content creation.
